pages.title: Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Technology Upgradation | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Technology Upgradation | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Technology Upgradation The objective of the Scheme is to facilitate technology up-gradation in MSEs by providing an upfront capital subsidy of 15 per cent (on institutional finance of upto Rs 1 crore availed by them) for induction of well-established and improved technology in the specified 51 sub-sectors/products approved. In other words the major objective is to upgrade their plant & machinery with state-of-the-art technology, with or without expansion and also for new MSEs which have set up their facilities with appropriate eligible and proven technology duly approved under scheme guidelines. pages.title: Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME | Job Mela | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME | Job Mela | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Job Mela Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME 11 June 2026 Mr. Rupesh Kumar 33 A Loan Mela was organized for trainees of self-employment programmes sponsored under the Assistance to Training Institutions (ATI) Scheme of the Ministry of MSME, Government of India. Representatives from 12 leading banks participated and interacted with 100+ aspiring entrepreneurs seeking financial assistance to start or scale up their businesses. Encouraged by the positive response, a larger-scale Loan Mela may be planned soon. nimsme MSMEs Entrepreneurship Job Mela News Categories Advanced Management Development Programs ATI Book Exhibition Collaborative Programme Entrepreneurship and Skill Development Programs Entrepreneurship Awareness Programmes General News Hackathon Inaugurations Indian Technical and Economic Cooperation Programmes (ITEC) International Conferences Job Mela Loan Mela Management Development Programs Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) National Announced Programs National Conference Official Visits Patents Press Meet SC ST Hub Schemes Seminar Special Occasions Sponsored Program Training of Trainers Valediction Workshops Hindi (हिन्दी) English Assamese (অসমীয়া) Bengali (বাংলা) Bodo (बड़ो) Dogri (डोगरी) Goan Konkani (गोवा कोंकणी) Gujarati (ગુજરાતી) Hindi (हिन्दी) Kannada (ಕನ್ನಡ) Kashmiri (کٲشُر) Maithili (मैथिली) Malayalam (മലയാളം) Manipuri (মণিপুরী) Marathi (मराठी) Nepali (नेपाली) Odia (ଓଡ଼ିଆ) Punjabi (ਪੰਜਾਬੀ) Sanskrit (संस्कृत) Santali (संताली) Sindhi (سنڌي) Tamil (தமிழ்) Telugu (తెలుగు) Urdu (اردو) Powered by इस अनुवाद का मूल्यांकन करें क्या आप प्रतिक्रिया देना चाहेंगे जमा करें इस पृष्ठ का अनुवाद भाषिणी के ए. आई.-संचालित मॉडल का उपयोग करके किया गया है। स्रोत सामग्री अंग्रेजी में है। अनुवाद से संबंधित किसी भी प्रश्न के लिए, कृपया ceo-dibd@digitalindia.gov.in से संपर्क करें।

pages.title: "Nari Shakti: Women Empowerment through Entrepreneurship & Leadership" workshop, conducted at ni-msme. Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME | Workshops | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: "Nari Shakti: Women Empowerment through Entrepreneurship & Leadership" workshop, conducted at ni-msme. Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME | Workshops | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Workshops "Nari Shakti: Women Empowerment through Entrepreneurship & Leadership" workshop, conducted at ni-msme. Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME 11 June 2026 Mr. Rupesh Kumar 50 The "Nari Shakti: Women Empowerment through Entrepreneurship & Leadership" workshop, sponsored by IDBI Bank, was held on 11 June 2026 at the ni-msme campus. The programme witnessed participation from 120 women entrepreneurs, professionals, aspiring entrepreneurs, and small business owners. Officials from 12 banks shared women-centric financial support schemes and funding opportunities to promote entrepreneurship and leadership among women. nimsme MSMEs Entrepreneurship Workshop Nari Shakti News Categories Advanced Management Development Programs एटीआई पुस्तक प्रदर्शनी सहयोगात्मक कार्यक्रम उद्यमिता और कौशल विकास कार्यक्रम उद्यमिता जागरूकता कार्यक्रम आम खबरें हैकाथॉन उद्घाटन समारोह भारतीय तकनीकी और आर्थिक सहयोग कार्यक्रम (आई. टी. ई. सी.) अंतर्राष्ट्रीय सम्मेलन नौकरी मेला ऋण मेला प्रबंधन विकास कार्यक्रम समझौता ज्ञापन (एमओयू) राष्ट्रीय घोषित कार्यक्रम राष्ट्रीय सम्मेलन आधिकारिक दौरे पेटेंट प्रेस मीट एससी एसटी हब योजनाएं सेमिनार विशेष अवसर प्रायोजित कार्यक्रम प्रशिक्षकों का प्रशिक्षण Valediction Workshops Hindi (हिन्दी) English Assamese (অসমীয়া) Bengali (বাংলা) Bodo (बड़ो) Dogri (डोगरी) Goan Konkani (गोवा कोंकणी) Gujarati (ગુજરાતી) Hindi (हिन्दी) Kannada (ಕನ್ನಡ) Kashmiri (کٲشُر) Maithili (मैथिली) Malayalam (മലയാളം) Manipuri (মণিপুরী) Marathi (मराठी) Nepali (नेपाली) Odia (ଓଡ଼ିଆ) Punjabi (ਪੰਜਾਬੀ) Sanskrit (संस्कृत) Santali (संताली) Sindhi (سنڌي) Tamil (தமிழ்) Telugu (తెలుగు) Urdu (اردو) Powered by Rate this translation Would you like to provide feedback Submit इस पृष्ठ का अनुवाद भाषिणी के ए. आई.-संचालित मॉडल का उपयोग करके किया गया है। स्रोत सामग्री अंग्रेजी में है। अनुवाद से संबंधित किसी भी प्रश्न के लिए, कृपया ceo-dibd@digitalindia.gov.in से संपर्क करें।

pages.title: MSME Schemes | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: MSME Schemes | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) श्री जीतन राम मांझी माननीय केंद्रीय एमएसएमई मंत्री सुश्री शोभा करंदलाजे माननीय राज्य मंत्री, एमएसएमई एमएसएमई योजनाएं नवाचार, ग्रामीण उद्योग और उद्यमिता को बढ़ावा देने के लिए एक योजना (एस्पायर) प्रशिक्षण संस्थानों को सहायता (ए. टी. आई.) योजना कॉयर विकास योजना-अम्ब्रेला योजना सूक्ष्म और लघु उद्यमों के लिए क्रेडिट गारंटी फंड ट्रस्ट (सी. जी. टी. एम. एस. ई.) दबावग्रस्त एमएसएमई के लिए अधीनस्थ ऋण के लिए ऋण गारंटी योजना (सी. जी. एस. एस. डी.) प्रौद्योगिकी उन्नयन के लिए ऋण से जुड़ी पूंजी सब्सिडी योजना उद्यमिता और कौशल विकास कार्यक्रम (ईएसडीपी) योजना अंतर्राष्ट्रीय सहयोग (आईसी) योजना खादी ग्रामोद्योग विकास योजना-छतरी योजना सूक्ष्म और लघु उद्यम समूह विकास कार्यक्रम (एमएसई-सीडीपी) योजना एमएसएमई चैंपियंस योजना राष्ट्रीय एससी-एसटी हब योजना प्रधानमंत्री रोजगार सृजन कार्यक्रम (पीएमईजीपी) खरीद और विपणन सहायता (पी. एम. एस.) योजना पूर्वोत्तर क्षेत्र और सिक्किम में एमएसएमई को बढ़ावा देना एमएसएमई प्रदर्शन को बढ़ाना और तेज करना (आरएएमपी) पारंपरिक उद्योगों के पुनर्जनन के लिए निधि की योजना (स्फूर्ति) आत्मनिर्भर भारत (एस. आर. आई.) कोष आत्मनिर्भर भारत के लिए एमएसएमई को सशक्त बनाना उपकरण कक्ष और तकनीकी संस्थान-अवसंरचना विकास और क्षमता निर्माण का एक घटक हमारी पत्रिकाएँ लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार pages.title: SELF RELIANT INDIA (SRI) FUND Empowering MSMEs for Aatmanirbhar Bharat | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: SELF RELIANT INDIA (SRI) FUND Empowering MSMEs for Aatmanirbhar Bharat | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME SELF RELIANT INDIA (SRI) FUND Empowering MSMEs for Aatmanirbhar Bharat About the scheme: • India has embarked upon the path of self reliant economic growth for achieving its aspiration of becoming an economic superpower. Towards this end, one of the initiatives taken by the Government of India is the launch of the Self Reliant India (SRI) Fund. • The Fund structure is designed in a manner that it will leverage the strength of the private sector in providing growth capital to viable MSMEs having a definite growth plan. Fund Objective: The Fund aims to provide capital support to the Daughter Funds for onward provision to MSMEs as growth capital, through equity, quasi-equity and debt to achieve the following: • Supporting faster growth of MSME businesses, thereby igniting the economy & creating several employment opportunities. • Supporting enterprises which have the potential to graduate beyond the MSME bracket and become National/International champions. • Supporting MSMEs which will help make India self-reliant by producing relevant technologies, goods and services. pages.title: Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries(SFURTI) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries(SFURTI) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries(SFURTI) Objective: •To organize traditional industries and artisans into collectives by increasing production and value addition to make products competitive •To promote traditional sectors and increase income of artisans providing sustainable employment. pages.title: Procurement and Marketing Support (PMS) Scheme | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Procurement and Marketing Support (PMS) Scheme | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Procurement and Marketing Support (PMS) Scheme Objective: •The scheme aims to promote new market access initiatives like organizing / participation in National / International Trade Fairs / Exhibitions / MSME Expo, etc. held across the country and to create awareness and educate the MSMEs about the importance / methods/ process of packaging in marketing, latest packaging technology, import-export policy and procedure, GeM portal, MSME Conclave, latest developments in international / national trade and other subjects / topics relevant for market access developments. pages.title: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) Availability of bank credit without the hassles of collaterals / third party guarantees would be a major source of support to the first generation entrepreneurs to realise their dream of setting up a unit of their own Micro and Small Enterprise (MSE). Keeping this objective in view, Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of India launched Credit Guarantee Scheme (CGS) so as to strengthen credit delivery system and facilitate flow of credit to the MSE sector. To operationalise the scheme, Government of India and SIDBI set up the Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE). CGTMSE has introduced a new "Hybrid Security" product allowing guarantee cover for the portion of credit facility not covered by collateral security. In the partial collateral security model, the MLIs will be allowed to obtain collateral security for a part of the credit facility, whereas the remaining part of the credit facility, up to a maximum of 200 lakh, can be covered under Credit Guarantee Scheme of CGTMSE. CGTMSE will, however, have pari-passu charge on the primary security as well as on the collateral security provided by the borrower for the credit facility. The main objective is that the lender should give importance to project viability and secure the credit facility purely on the primary security of the assets financed. The other objective is that the lender availing guarantee facility should endeavor to give composite credit to the borrowers so that the borrowers obtain both term loan and working capital facilities from a single agency. The Credit Guarantee scheme (CGS) seeks to reassure the lender that, in the event of a MSE unit, which availed collateral free credit facilities, fails to discharge its liabilities to the lender, the Guarantee Trust would make good the loss incurred by the lender up to 50/75/80/85 per cent of the credit facility. pages.title: About us | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: About us | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Vision To play an active role in MSME policy formulation and implementation in priority sectors aligned with Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Mission To emerge as Centre of Excellence in Innovation and Entrepreneurship for sustainable growth of MSMEs while conforming to national priorities for attaining global standards. About ni-msme ni-msme , the pioneer institute of MSME , is playing a transformational role in providing a pro-business environment to foster the progress of MSMEs . The raison detre of this Institute is to assist the Government in formulating policies for micro, small and medium enterprises and to help the practicing and potential entrepreneurs through a bouquet of services like training, research, consultancy, information, education and extension. The Institute is a training ground for middle and senior level technocrats, bureaucrats, bankers, potential entrepreneurs, trainers, educationists and Civil Society Organizations who come here to gain knowledge and expertise for keeping abreast of cutting-edge management practices and streamline their operations. Set up in 1960, ni-msme has made valuable contributions by creating an impressive record of achievements beyond the Indian shores, enabling other developing countries to access the benefit of the Institute’s expertise and facilities. ni-msme’s initiatives are dynamic and futuristic for meeting the needs of enterprises in the context of changing Global Economic Scenarios. Located in a sprawling and enthralling campus amidst a rich natural setting in Hyderabad, ni-msme is well equipped with physical and academic infrastructure. In keeping with the changing times and technological advances, the Institute has revolutionized its style of functioning by adopting technology in every facet of its mandate, even while retaining the wisdom and advantages of deeply ingrained management practices. Our Incubation Center nucleates a cluster of new age ventures. The Institute collects and disseminates information that helps make a successful entrepreneur who is well versed in the intricacies of business and can participate in business activities intelligently and diligently. The Small Enterprises National Documentation Centre (SENDOC) facilitates this process. The institute has recently commemorated the Diamond Jubilee, 60 years of commendable services to MSME sector and the country. The Director General Dr. Ashutosh A. pages.title: Our Genesis | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Our Genesis | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Our Genesis 2022 Diamond Jubilee The institute commemorated its Diamond Jubilee, 60 years of commendable services to MSME sector and the country. 2012 Golden Jubilee The Institute celebrated its Golden Jubilee Year. 2007 Rechristened as ni-msme The Institute has earned distinction in entrepreneurship promotion, with recognition at national and international levels. With the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Development Act of 2006 (MSME-D) the Institute was renamed as National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) with re-designed innovative focus of inclusive growth under the Ministry of MSME. 1984 UNIDO Recognition The UNIDO recognizing the outstanding performance of SIET under the scheme of Centers of Excellence, for its meritorious performance accorded the national status and renamed as National Institute for Small Industries Extension and Training (nisiet) in 1984. 1962 Renaming the Institute The Institute was renamed as Small Industry Extension Training Institute (SIET) shifting the headquarters to Hyderabad in 1962. 1960 The Foundation Central Industrial Extension Training Institute (CIETI) took birth with Micro Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) as a Department under the Ministry of Industry and Commerce in New Delhi. In order to provide greater autonomy in administration and procedures to play a pivotal role in the promotion of MSMEs. pages.title: Dr. Ashutosh A. Murkute | Director General | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Dr. Ashutosh A. Murkute | Director General | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Director General Dr. Ashutosh A. Murkute Director General Profile Blog posts Publications Awards Profile Dr. Ashutosh A. Murkute, assumed the charge of In-charge Director General, National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) on 19 March 2025. After completing Ph.D. from I.I.T., Delhi he started research career as Scientist ‘C’ at DRDO – Defence Institute of High Altitude Research (DIHAR, DRDO), Leh-Ladakh. Besides developing agro-technologies suitable for high altitude, he established a prototype for solar thermal greenhouse and an experimental tissue culture lab at Leh. Dr. Murkute (Senior Scientist), worked on post-harvest management of onion and garlic at ICAR – Directorate of Onion and Garlic Research, Pune. He established a protocol for cold – storage of onion vis-à-vis inherent potential of different cultivars. He also developed a complete protocol for production of virus-free planting material of garlic through microbulbiles using mericlones and established a tissue culture lab for the mass production. At ICAR – Central Citrus Research Institute, Nagpur, Dr. Murkute (Principal Scientist), worked on citrus improvement program and also projects on abiotic stress management. His team released three citrus varieties i.e. pummelo (Citrus grandis) cv. NRCC Pummelo-5, grapefruit (Citrus paradisi) cv. Flame and Sweet Orange (Citrus sinensis) cv. Cutter Valencia through State Variety Release Committee (SVRC), Maharashtra and a grapefruit (Citrus paradisi) cv. NRCC Grapefruit -6 through institute for commercial cultivation. He published research articles in high rated peer reviewed journals of international repute on high-density panting, organic cultivation, tissue culture etc. There are many books, book-chapters, popular articles etc. to his credit. As a Chairman of Works Committee he completed civil works of Rs. 500 lakhs and through project of revolving fund scheme he earned revenue of more than Rs 700 lakhs by selling quality disease-free citrus plants of acid lime, mandarin, sweet orange and other improved citrus cultivars at CCRI, Nagpur. As PI/ Co-PI of externally funded competitive grants he handled research project of more than Rs 3000 lakhs, besides institute funded projects. Appointments such as Adjunct Professor at I.I.T. Delhi; Member, Board of Management of Rajmata Vijaya Raje Scindia Agricultural University, Gwalior; Member, Academic Council of Nagpur University, Member, Management Committees of ICAR institutes’, Member, Board of Studies, Central Agriculture University, Imphal are some of the prestigious recognitions for his illustrious career. SENDOC is not just repository of books, but also a place of learning, research and community engagement which includes Collections - Wide range of collections that include books, magazines, newspapers and digital materials. Also have special collections that focus on Entrepreneurship and MSME. pages.title: Digital Classrooms | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Digital Classrooms | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Close Digital Classrooms Digital classrooms have become a popular way of learning, especially during the current pandemic. Here are some ideas for content that can be used in digital classrooms: Interactive quizzes and assessments - These can be used to test students' knowledge and understanding of a particular subject. They can also be used for formative assessments and to identify areas where students may need additional support. Video lectures and tutorials - These can be used to provide students with a more engaging and visual way of learning. They can be used to introduce new topics, reinforce concepts, or provide additional explanations. Virtual field trips - These can be used to take students on a virtual tour of a particular location or to experience a particular event. They can be used to provide students with a more immersive learning experience and to help them develop a deeper understanding of a particular subject. Collaborative projects - These can be used to encourage students to work together and to develop important skills such as communication, collaboration, and problem-solving. Projects can be based on a particular topic or theme and can be completed using online tools such as Google Docs or Padlet. Digital textbooks and resources - These can be used to provide students with access to a wide range of learning materials, including textbooks, articles, and videos. They can be used to supplement classroom instruction and to provide students with additional resources and materials to support their learning. These are just a few ideas for content that can be used in digital classrooms. By using a variety of different formats and approaches, teachers can create engaging and effective learning experiences for their students. pages.title: Recreation | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Recreation | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Close Recreation Recreation facilities at ni-msme are essential for promoting health and wellness among participants, students and staff. Here are some common recreation facilities that can be found at ni-msme : Fitness centre: ni-msme have fitness center that is equipped with weight training equipment, cardio machines, and group fitness classes. These facilities are designed to help students and staff maintain an active and healthy lifestyle. Sports courts and fields: ni-msme campus provides Badminton Court, Tennis Court, Football Ground for organized sports teams or for casual play that allow students and staff to participate in organized sports leagues and tournaments. A great way to stay active and meet new people on campus. Recreation facilities are important for promoting physical health and wellness, as well as providing a sense of community and social engagement. By providing access to these facilities, ni-msme can help students and staff lead healthy and fulfilling lives both on and off campus. हमारी पत्रिकाएँ लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) प्रमुख समाचार "नारी शक्तिः उद्यमिता और नेतृत्व के माध्यम से महिला सशक्तिकरण" 11 June 2026 उद्यम की आकांक्षा-ए. टी. आई. योजना .......... के तहत एन. आई. एम. एस. एम. ई. में आयोजित ऋण मेला 11 June 2026 ऑगस्टस गूसी के नेतृत्व में घाना के प्रतिनिधिमंडल ने एमएसएमई का पता लगाने के लिए एनआई-एमएसएमई का दौरा किया 10 June 2026 एन. आई.-एम. एस. एम. ई. ने वाइब्रेंट के लिए रणनीतिक हस्तक्षेप तैयार करने पर ए-एम. डी. पी. का उद्घाटन किया 08 June 2026 एक विशेषज्ञ स्तर पर प्रशिक्षक कार्यशाला को भाप प्रणाली अनुकूलन पर प्रशिक्षित करें 08 June 2026 एन. आई.-एम. एस. एम. ई. ने एम. एस. एम. ई. ऑनलाइन विवाद समाधान को मजबूत करने पर एक कार्यशाला का आयोजन किया 04 June 2026 Hindi (हिन्दी) English Assamese (অসমীয়া) Bengali (বাংলা) Bodo (बड़ो) Dogri (डोगरी) Goan Konkani (गोवा कोंकणी) Gujarati (ગુજરાતી) Hindi (हिन्दी) Kannada (ಕನ್ನಡ) Kashmiri (کٲشُر) Maithili (मैथिली) Malayalam (മലയാളം) Manipuri (মণিপুরী) Marathi (मराठी) Nepali (नेपाली) Odia (ଓଡ଼ିଆ) Punjabi (ਪੰਜਾਬੀ) Sanskrit (संस्कृत) Santali (संताली) Sindhi (سنڌي) Tamil (தமிழ்) Telugu (తెలుగు) Urdu (اردو) Powered by इस अनुवाद का मूल्यांकन करें क्या आप प्रतिक्रिया देना चाहेंगे जमा करें इस पृष्ठ का अनुवाद भाषिणी के ए. आई.-संचालित मॉडल का उपयोग करके किया गया है। स्रोत सामग्री अंग्रेजी में है। अनुवाद से संबंधित किसी भी प्रश्न के लिए, कृपया ceo-dibd@digitalindia.gov.in से संपर्क करें।

pages.title: School of Enterprise Development (SED) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: School of Enterprise Development (SED) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME School of Enterprise Development (SED) About School It is one of the former scholastic/intellectual departments of ni-msme, continued with additional responsibilities in the globalised market environment. MSME development generally refers to facilitating progress in the economy and generation of employment by enhancing business development services, and establishing a conducive legal, regulatory and policy environment. In this context, the School of Enterprise Development [SED] acts as a catalyst for dynamic, integrated change in Micro and Small Enterprise (MSE) sector, looking strategically and optimistically towards the future challenges. The activities of the SED include programme evaluation studies, research projects, feasibility studies, potential surveys, rural enterprise promotion, backward area development, infrastructure development programme and project implementation for central and state governments, that advances an innovative and enterprise growth in the global and national scenario. On the international front, the SED provides a bridge between theory and practice of small business and MSE development by guiding/training the executives of MSME in formulation, development, implementation and evaluation of various facets of enterprise policy, with a focus to make the MSE sector more competitive in the global economic scene. Besides imparting training to the officials, the experienced and well-known faculty of the school focus on economic surveys in the nature of diagnosing the industrial potential, networking with national and international agencies, and other research studies pertaining to government programmes /schemes and impact studies. The School of Enterprise Development functions through Centre for Enterprise Planning and Development (C-EPD), Centre for Policy Research (C-PR), National Resource Centre for Cluster Development (NRCD), ni-msme NGO Network (N-Cube) and Economic Investigation and Statistical Cell (EISC) in the areas of Cluster development, Employment Generation, and establishing synergy with related departments, which of late became the centres of excellence through focused attention for holistic development. pages.title: School of Enterprise Management (SEM) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: School of Enterprise Management (SEM) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME School of Enterprise Management (SEM) About School This is a well-established academic organ of ni-msme. There is a spate of changes sweeping the enterprises across the globe. The economic reforms within the country with the attendant consequences, ever increasing competition, both global and local, fast pace of technological changes and a host of other factors necessitate revolutionary reforms in the enterprise management practices. The practices clearly have to be cost-effective as well as simpler to adopt and adapt with the power to impinge all segments of the enterprise. This calls for a paradigm shift in raison d’etre of the enterprise itself. In tune with this, the erstwhile Industrial Management Division is re-engineered and re-christened as School of Enterprise Management (SEM) laying emphasis on management practices, intellectual property rights and management education. The School is endowed with capabilities in the following areas and delivers through the instruments of training and consultancy, many a time customised to meet the requirements of client organisation, actively supported by research. The School functions with its full-fledged five centres viz., Centre for Promotion of Advanced Management Practices (C-PAMP), Centre for Logistics & Integrated Materials Systems (C-LAIMS), Centre for Industrial Credit and Financial Services (C-ICFS) and Centre for Intellectual Property Rights (C-IPR) and Centre for Environment Concerns (C-Eco). pages.title: School of Entrepreneurship and Extension (SEE) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: School of Entrepreneurship and Extension (SEE) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) श्री जीतन राम मांझी माननीय केंद्रीय एमएसएमई मंत्री सुश्री शोभा करंदलाजे माननीय राज्य मंत्री, एमएसएमई उद्यमिता और विस्तार विद्यालय (एस. ई. ई.) स्कूल के बारे में स्कूल ऑफ एंटरप्रेन्योरशिप एंड एक्सटेंशन समकालीन एमएसएमई प्राथमिकताओं के साथ क्षमता निर्माण, नीति आउटरीच और जमीनी स्तर पर उद्यमिता को बढ़ावा देने के लिए एक गतिशील केंद्र के रूप में विकसित हुआ है। स्कूल एमएसएमई नीति कार्यान्वयन, व्यवसाय विकास रणनीतियों, विपणन, परियोजना योजना और उद्यमिता को शामिल करते हुए अभ्यास-उन्मुख, बहु-अनुशासनात्मक प्रशिक्षण कार्यक्रमों को डिजाइन करने और वितरित करने में सक्रिय रूप से लगा हुआ है, जो अक्सर नवाचार पारिस्थितिकी तंत्र के साथ एकीकृत होता है। इसके अलावा, एस. ई. ई. ने उद्यमिता जागरूकता, आउटरीच कार्यक्रमों और संस्थागत सहयोग में महत्वपूर्ण भूमिका निभाई है, जिसमें एमएसएमई पारिस्थितिकी तंत्र को मजबूत करने और विशेष रूप से युवाओं, महिलाओं, एसएचजी और हाशिए पर रहने वाले समुदायों के बीच समावेशी विकास को बढ़ावा देने के लिए राज्य एजेंसियों और वित्तीय संस्थानों के साथ साझेदारी शामिल है। स्कूल के हस्तक्षेप उद्यमिता विकास और स्थानीय उद्यम निर्माण पर तेजी से जोर देते हैं, जिसमें मजदूरी रोजगार और सूक्ष्म उद्यम दोनों पैदा करने के लिए जिलों में प्रशिक्षण बैच आयोजित किए जा रहे हैं। इसके अलावा, एस. ई. ई. विशेषज्ञ व्याख्यानों, सम्मेलनों और परामर्श कार्यक्रमों के माध्यम से राष्ट्रीय और अंतर्राष्ट्रीय ज्ञान के प्रसार में योगदान देता है, जो नवाचार प्रसार, प्रौद्योगिकी हस्तांतरण और सतत उद्यम विकास जैसे उभरते क्षेत्रों को संबोधित करता है। इसके कार्यक्रमों की विशेषता संवादात्मक शिक्षाशास्त्र, मामले-आधारित शिक्षा, क्षेत्र में भागीदारी, व्यावहारिक परिणाम सुनिश्चित करना और संस्थागत क्षमताओं में वृद्धि है। कुल मिलाकर, एस. ई. ई. उद्यमशीलता की क्षमताओं को बढ़ावा देकर, शासन क्षमताओं को मजबूत करके और समावेशी और टिकाऊ एम. एस. एम. ई. विकास को सक्षम करके "विकसित भारत" के दृष्टिकोण के साथ अपनी गतिविधियों को संरेखित करना जारी रखता है। इसके घटक केंद्र इस प्रकार हैंः 1. उद्यमिता विकास प्रकोष्ठ 2. आजीविका व्यवसाय ऊष्मायन प्रकोष्ठ निर्देशक डॉ. श्रीकांत शर्मा निर्देशक संकाय श्री वी. बी. राजेंद्र प्रसाद संकाय सदस्य डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार संकाय सदस्य श्री गुडेल देवन साई फैकल्टी एसोसिएट कार्यक्रम सभी कार्यक्रम आने वाला है जारी है ग्रामीण उद्यमों के लिए मूल्य श्रृंखला विकास पर प्रशिक्षण कार्यक्रम डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार 22 June 2026 ऑनलाइन वैज्ञानिक मधुमक्खी पालन पर प्रशिक्षण कार्यक्रम तेल के बीजों के साथ एकीकृत डॉ. श्रीकांत शर्मा 18 June 2026 नी-मिमे अभिनव शिक्षण और सीखने पर ऑनलाइन संकाय विकास कार्यक्रम (एफ. डी. पी.) डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार 01 September 2025 ऑनलाइन टिकाऊ .......... के लिए प्रभावी कार्यशील पूंजी प्रबंधन और ऋण संबंध डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार 28 July 2025 ऑनलाइन उद्यमिता और कौशल विकास में प्रशिक्षकों (टीओटी) का प्रशिक्षण डॉ. श्रीकांत शर्मा 21 July 2025 नी-मिमे ग्रामीण क्षेत्रों के कायाकल्प के लिए सूक्ष्म उद्यमों पर अंतर्राष्ट्रीय प्रशिक्षण कार्यक्रम डॉ. श्रीकांत शर्मा 23 February 2025 नी-मिमे स्टार्टअप को सशक्त बनानाः नवाचार, विकास और विकास के लिए एमएसएमई योजनाओं में महारत हासिल करना डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार 20 February 2025 ऑनलाइन भारत में किसान उत्पादक कंपनियों (एफ. पी. सी.) को सशक्त बनानाः .......... के माध्यम से विकास को चालू करना डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार 20 January 2025 ऑनलाइन समाचार श्रेणियाँ उन्नत प्रबंधन विकास कार्यक्रम एटीआई पुस्तक प्रदर्शनी सहयोगात्मक कार्यक्रम उद्यमिता और कौशल विकास कार्यक्रम उद्यमिता जागरूकता कार्यक्रम आम खबरें हैकाथॉन उद्घाटन समारोह भारतीय तकनीकी और आर्थिक सहयोग कार्यक्रम (आई. टी. ई. सी.) अंतर्राष्ट्रीय सम्मेलन नौकरी मेला ऋण मेला प्रबंधन विकास कार्यक्रम समझौता ज्ञापन (एमओयू) राष्ट्रीय घोषित कार्यक्रम राष्ट्रीय सम्मेलन आधिकारिक दौरे पेटेंट प्रेस मीट एससी एसटी हब योजनाएं सेमिनार विशेष अवसर प्रायोजित कार्यक्रम प्रशिक्षकों का प्रशिक्षण मोक्ष प्राप्ति कार्यशालाएँ हमारी पत्रिकाएँ लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और pages.title: School of Enterprise Information and Communication (SEIC) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: School of Enterprise Information and Communication (SEIC) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME School of Enterprise Information and Communication (SEIC) About School The School of Information and Communication (SEIC) is a well established unique knowledge hub of ni-msme in providing information services and ICT solutions through training to both the national and international MSME community. To strengthen the informational base of MSMEs in facing the global challenges led to the formation of School of Enterprise Information and Communication (SEIC) laying emphasis on providing information services in varied forms and need based skill oriented Information Technology (IT) training using the latest technologies. The school functions with its fully equipped sources of information and state-of-the-art infrastructure to meet the informational needs and ICT skill development training requirements of MSMEs, Government organisations, public and private sectors, NGOs, entrepreneurs, students, researchers etc. through its two Centres viz, Small Enterprises National Documentation Centre (SENDOC) and Centre for Communication and Information Technology (C-CIT). pages.title: National Resource Centre for Cluster Development (NRCD) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: National Resource Centre for Cluster Development (NRCD) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME National Resource Centre for Cluster Development (NRCD) The National Resource Centre for Cluster Development (NRCD), established in 2004 at ni-msme with support from the Development Commissioner (MSME), was created to strengthen the competitiveness of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) through a cluster-based development approach. This approach addresses key challenges faced by MSMEs such as isolation, lack of technology, weak market linkages, and limited access to skills and information. The primary objective of NRCD is to promote and implement cluster development across India by providing end-to-end support to MSME clusters. Its core functions include cluster identification, diagnostic studies, preparation of detailed project reports, strategic intervention design, capacity building, and continuous monitoring and handholding. It also facilitates technology upgradation, infrastructure development, and deployment of business development services to improve productivity and competitiveness. NRCD plays a crucial role in implementing government initiatives like the SFURTI scheme, supporting multiple clusters across states. Through these interventions, it enhances artisan incomes, promotes innovation, strengthens market access, and ensures sustainable enterprise development. Overall, NRCD acts as a national-level catalyst for transforming MSMEs into competitive, collaborative, and growth-oriented ecosystems. pages.title: Intellectual Property Facilitation Centre (IPFC) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Intellectual Property Facilitation Centre (IPFC) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Intellectual Property Facilitation Centre (IPFC) The Intellectual Property Facilitation Center (IPFC) at the National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) , Hyderabad, is a dedicated resource center established to assist MSMEs, startups, entrepreneurs, academic institutions, and innovators in leveraging the value of Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs). With the primary objective of strengthening the IP ecosystem in India, the IPFC at ni-msme provides end-to-end support for patents, trademarks, copyrights, industrial designs, and geographical indications . The center aims to promote awareness, build capacity, and facilitate protection and commercialization of innovations among MSMEs Key Services Offered : IP Awareness & Capacity Building : Organizing workshops, seminars, training programs, and webinars on IPR for MSMEs, startups, students, and faculty. IPR Consultancy : Guidance on patentability assessment, prior art search, filing strategies, trademark protection, and design registrations. Filing Support : End-to-end assistance in drafting and filing of patents, trademarks, copyrights, and design applications with Indian IP offices. Commercialization & Monetization : Advising on IP valuation, technology transfer, licensing, and strategies to generate revenue from IP assets. The IPFC operates under the guidance of experienced professionals, registered patent and trademark agents, and domain experts. It serves as a bridge between innovation and market realization by empowering MSMEs to protect and capitalize on their creative and innovative work. pages.title: Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) The Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) at the National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) is committed to fostering a vibrant and inclusive entrepreneurial ecosystem. The Cell plays a pivotal role in promoting entrepreneurship through comprehensive capacity building, mentoring, handholding support, and enterprise facilitation services for both aspiring and existing entrepreneurs. Objectives To promote entrepreneurship as a viable and sustainable career option among youth, women, and marginalized communities. To strengthen MSMEs through skill development, innovation, and enterprise support services. To facilitate adoption of technology-driven and environmentally sustainable business practices. Core Functions and Activities 1. Entrepreneurship Development Programmes (EDPs) EDC designs and delivers structured training programmes focusing on business planning, financial literacy, market linkages, regulatory compliance, and the use of digital tools for enterprise growth. 2. Entrepreneurship Awareness Programmes (EAPs) These programmes aim to sensitize students and potential entrepreneurs about emerging business opportunities, Government schemes, and the evolving startup ecosystem. 3. Skill Development & Capacity Building The Cell conducts sector-specific skill development initiatives to enhance employability and build entrepreneurial competencies aligned with market demand. 4. Incubation & Handholding Support EDC provides end-to-end support including idea validation, preparation of project reports, assistance in business registration, facilitation of access to finance, and continuous mentoring through incubation initiatives. 5. Facilitation of Government Schemes The Cell actively supports entrepreneurs in accessing various flagship schemes such as Prime Minister’s Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP), Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (MUDRA), Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) etc. 6. Innovation & Startup Promotion EDC promotes innovation-driven enterprises by supporting startups through mentoring, networking, and linkages with industry, academia, and research institutions. pages.title: Centre for Financial Inclusion (CFI), FinRise | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Centre for Financial Inclusion (CFI), FinRise | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Centre for Financial Inclusion (CFI), FinRise Centre of Financial Inclusion is established at ni-msme with the support from SIDBI and CGTMSE. Objectives of CFI: To sensitise the CGTMSE & MLIs (Member Lending Institutions) on issues faced by MSEs at the field level. To create awareness on financial literacy and credit counselling to MSEs, thus helping them to better utilize the benefits of the CGTMSE Scheme. To disseminate knowledge on financial prudence and tailored best practices to Progressive entrepreneurs for a better financial mechanism. To develop formal and systematic credit avenues for the financially disadvantaged progressive entrepreneurs throughout their transformational journey to medium scale. Purpose Statement “To be Centre of Excellence in catering to MSEs needs to enhance their creditworthiness and promote Governance and financial management among MSEs through democratizing financial knowledge & best practices, inculcating financial discipline and prudence in MSEs ecosystem”. Role of SIDBI As principal Financial Institution for executing the triple agenda of promotion, financing and development of the MSME sector and coordination of the functions of the various Institutions engaged in similar activities. Role of CGTMSE To extend the support to progressive entrepreneurs who are having bankable business ideas but lack collateral security and/or third-party guarantee to access credit from formal source by helping them to provide the guarantee to enable them access credit leading to setting up viable micro and small enterprises. Thereby transforming them from job seekers to job providers and meaningfully contribute to nation building. Role of ni-msme Being a pioneer Entrepreneurship Development Institute, will establish a Chair in CFI and will carry out research and consultancy activities for identifying the credit gaps and need analysis for MSMEs and build the capacity of all stakeholders under CGTMSE spectrum for greater utilization of CGTMSE benefits. हमारी पत्रिकाएँ लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) प्रमुख समाचार "नारी शक्तिः उद्यमिता और नेतृत्व के माध्यम से महिला सशक्तिकरण" 11 June 2026 उद्यम की आकांक्षा-ए. टी. आई. योजना .......... के तहत एन. आई. एम. एस. एम. ई. में आयोजित ऋण मेला 11 June 2026 ऑगस्टस गूसी के नेतृत्व में घाना के प्रतिनिधिमंडल ने एमएसएमई का पता लगाने के लिए एनआई-एमएसएमई का दौरा किया 10 June 2026 एन. आई.-एम. एस. एम. ई. ने वाइब्रेंट के लिए रणनीतिक हस्तक्षेप तैयार करने पर ए-एम. डी. पी. का उद्घाटन किया 08 June 2026 एक विशेषज्ञ स्तर पर प्रशिक्षक कार्यशाला को भाप प्रणाली अनुकूलन पर प्रशिक्षित करें 08 June 2026 एन. आई.-एम. एस. एम. ई. ने एम. एस. एम. ई. ऑनलाइन विवाद समाधान को मजबूत करने पर एक कार्यशाला का आयोजन किया 04 June 2026 Hindi (हिन्दी) English Assamese (অসমীয়া) Bengali (বাংলা) Bodo (बड़ो) Dogri (डोगरी) Goan Konkani (गोवा कोंकणी) Gujarati (ગુજરાતી) Hindi (हिन्दी) Kannada (ಕನ್ನಡ) Kashmiri (کٲشُر) Maithili (मैथिली) Malayalam (മലയാളം) Manipuri (মণিপুরী) Marathi (मराठी) Nepali (नेपाली) Odia (ଓଡ଼ିଆ) Punjabi (ਪੰਜਾਬੀ) Sanskrit (संस्कृत) Santali (संताली) Sindhi (سنڌي) Tamil (தமிழ்) Telugu (తెలుగు) Urdu (اردو) Powered by इस अनुवाद का मूल्यांकन करें क्या आप प्रतिक्रिया देना चाहेंगे जमा करें इस पृष्ठ का अनुवाद भाषिणी के ए. आई.-संचालित मॉडल का उपयोग करके किया गया है। स्रोत सामग्री अंग्रेजी में है। अनुवाद से संबंधित किसी भी प्रश्न के लिए, कृपया ceo-dibd@digitalindia.gov.in से संपर्क करें।

M i c r o  S m a l l  a n d  M e d i u m  E n t e r p r i s e s  ( n i - m s m e )
An Organisation of Ministry of MSME, Govt. of India and ISO 9001:2015 Certified
Accredited as UTKRISHT / उ त्क ृ ष्ट , 3-Star Rating by Capacity Building Commission (CBC)
International Labour Organization (ILO) Women's Entrepreneurship (WE) Check Certified
The Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) are the undisputed  architects of India’s
journey toward a $5 trillion economy and the overarching vision of Viks it Bharat @ 2047. As
we transition into FY 2026-27, the MSME ecosystem is undergoing a pro found structural shift,
driven by digital integration, green manufacturing, and enhanced global competitiveness.
The Union Budget 2026-27 has furt her fortified this sector by placing MSMEs at the core of
India’s growth strategy. With the en hancement of Credit Guarantee covers to ₹ 10 crore, the
introduction of customized credit cards for micro-enterprises, and the operationalization of
the SME Growth Fund, the focus has shifted from mere survival to aggressive scaling and
equity support. Furthermore, initiatives like PM Vishwakarma and RAMP are bridging the gap
between traditional craftsmanship and modern industrial standards, ensuring an inclusive and
resilient value chain.
In alignment with these national priorities, ni-msme has curated the National Executive
Development Programmes for FY 2026-27. Our training calendar is meticulously designed to
address the emerging demands of I ndustry 5.0, including Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain in
supply chains, and Sustainable Practices like Green Hydrogen and ZED (Zero Defect Zero
Effect) certification. We aim to empower entrepreneurs, policymakers, and financial executives
with the tools to leverage platforms like ONDC and TReDS, facilitating seamless market access
and liquidity.
Our sprawling, world-class campus in Hyderabad continues to serve as a hub for innovation,
offering state-of-the-art academic infrastructure and a rich alumni network for global
experience sharing. This year, we are proud to present a comprehensive suite of over 68
capacity-building programs, ranging from IPR and Digital Marketing to Cluster Development
and International Trade.
I invite all stakeholders to engage with these programs and harness the expertise at ni-msme
to transform challenges into sustainable business advantages. Together, let us build a globally
competitive and innovation-led MSME sector.
Dr. Ashutosh A. Murkute
Director General
F o r e w o r d

About
The National Institute for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme), Hyderabad, stands as a beacon
of entrepreneurial excellence, fostering innovation, capacity building, and sustainable business growth. As a
premier institution under the Ministry of Micro, Sm all & Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of Indi a,
ni-msme plays a pivotal role in shaping the future of India's entrepreneurial landscape. With a rich l egacy of
empowering enterprises, the institute is committed to enabling MSMEs to become competitive, resilient,
and globally integrated, in alignment with the Viksit Bharat 2047 vision.
At the heart of ni-msme’s mission is a dynamic appr oach to policy research, skill development, and
enterprise facilitation. The institute offers cutti ng-edge training programs, consultancy services, an d
strategic interventions that address the evolving n eeds of MSMEs. By integrating advanced technology,
sustainable practices, and global best standards, n i-msme nurtures a new generation of business leader s
who contribute to India’s transformation into a knowledge-driven economy.
With decades of expertise in entrepreneurship devel opment, financial management, digital transformatio n,
and global market integration, ni-msme stands as a center of excellence for MSMEs. The institute
specializes in policy advisory, capacity building, and research-driven innovation, ensuring enterprise s are
equipped with the necessary skills to thrive in a r apidly evolving economic landscape. By leveraging d igital
tools, artificial intelligence, and green technolog ies, ni-msme facilitates sustainable business pract ices that
align with India’s vision of becoming a developed economy by 2047.
In line with Viksit Bharat 2047, ni-msme envisions a future where MSMEs serve as the backbone of a sel f-
reliant and digitally empowered India. The institut e actively collaborates with national and internati onal
stakeholders to enhance market access, promote digi tal transformation, and foster inclusive growth.
Through industry-academia linkages, research initia tives, and policy advocacy, ni-msme ensures that
MSMEs are equipped to seize emerging opportunities in global trade and innovation.
The institute’s state-of-the-art infrastructure, ex perienced faculty, and an extensive network of indu stry
partnerships create an enabling ecosystem for enter prise development. With specialized programs in
financial literacy, export promotion, and technological advancement, ni-msme is committed to unlocking the
full potential of Indian businesses, making them gl obally competitive and future-ready. As India strid es
toward Viksit Bharat 2047, ni-msme remains steadfas t in its mission to drive entrepreneurial excellenc e,
economic empowerment, and inclusive development. By  fostering a culture of innovation, adaptability, a nd
sustainability, ni-msme continues to be a catalyst for transforming MSMEs into growth engines of a
progressive, self-sufficient, and globally recognized India.

ni-msme has four theme focused Schools of expertise, under which Centres of Excellence are
functioning synergistically to pursue specific activities. All th ese Centres have faculty expertise
and are equipped to conduct demand driven and customi zed collaborative programmes for the
client organizations. The faculty is ably supported by a group of Consultants, Technical &
Administrative Staff and empaneled Sectoral Experts.
The lush green campus with   accommodation annexes and study blocks, stretches of ve lvet
lawns and crowning trees, presents a picturesque setti ng easy on the eye and soothing to the
mind, making intellectual pursuit an enjoyable avocatio n. The dining complex contains well
maintained, spacious and air conditioned dining halls  equipped with modern kitchen facilities for
multi-cuisine cooking and serving which caters to 300 participants.
Functional Structure
At ni-msme, world-class training infrastructure seaml essly blends interactive learning with
cutting-edge applications. The institute houses two t raining complexes, namely “Training Block”
and “Diamond Jubilee Bhawan”, with the former one fea turing 14 well-equipped lecture halls, a
conference hall with a 125-seat capacity, and a state-of -the-art auditorium accommodating 300
participants. The “Diamond Jubilee Bhawan” was inaugurat ed in 2022 as part of its expansion
strategy, setting new benchmarks with industry-aligne d facilities, ensuring an immersive and
holistic learning experience consisting of 21 classrooms , 2 seminar halls – capacity of 150
participants each, and 1 computer lab. The central l ibrary, a treasure trove of 45,000 books and
8,200 journals, serves as an intellectual hub, while ded icated interaction rooms foster syndicate
discussions, brainstorming, and collaborative learning.  The entire training complex is Wi-Fi
enabled, providing seamless high-speed internet acce ss. Nestled in serene, sylvan
surroundings, the campus offers an enriching learning ambiance, where creativity and innovation
thrive at every corner. International participants un dergo a specialized induction program,
designed to blend Indian learning paradigms with cultu ral insights, ensuring deeper assimilation
of training concepts and their global applications. At ni-msme, excellence meets inspiration,
shaping future-ready leaders in the MSME ecosystem.

pages.title: Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Technology Upgradation | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Technology Upgradation | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Technology Upgradation The objective of the Scheme is to facilitate technology up-gradation in MSEs by providing an upfront capital subsidy of 15 per cent (on institutional finance of upto Rs 1 crore availed by them) for induction of well-established and improved technology in the specified 51 sub-sectors/products approved. In other words the major objective is to upgrade their plant & machinery with state-of-the-art technology, with or without expansion and also for new MSEs which have set up their facilities with appropriate eligible and proven technology duly approved under scheme guidelines. pages.title: Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME | Job Mela | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME | Job Mela | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Job Mela Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME 11 June 2026 Mr. Rupesh Kumar 33 A Loan Mela was organized for trainees of self-employment programmes sponsored under the Assistance to Training Institutions (ATI) Scheme of the Ministry of MSME, Government of India. Representatives from 12 leading banks participated and interacted with 100+ aspiring entrepreneurs seeking financial assistance to start or scale up their businesses. Encouraged by the positive response, a larger-scale Loan Mela may be planned soon. nimsme MSMEs Entrepreneurship Job Mela News Categories Advanced Management Development Programs ATI Book Exhibition Collaborative Programme Entrepreneurship and Skill Development Programs Entrepreneurship Awareness Programmes General News Hackathon Inaugurations Indian Technical and Economic Cooperation Programmes (ITEC) International Conferences Job Mela Loan Mela Management Development Programs Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) National Announced Programs National Conference Official Visits Patents Press Meet SC ST Hub Schemes Seminar Special Occasions Sponsored Program Training of Trainers Valediction Workshops Hindi (हिन्दी) English Assamese (অসমীয়া) Bengali (বাংলা) Bodo (बड़ो) Dogri (डोगरी) Goan Konkani (गोवा कोंकणी) Gujarati (ગુજરાતી) Hindi (हिन्दी) Kannada (ಕನ್ನಡ) Kashmiri (کٲشُر) Maithili (मैथिली) Malayalam (മലയാളം) Manipuri (মণিপুরী) Marathi (मराठी) Nepali (नेपाली) Odia (ଓଡ଼ିଆ) Punjabi (ਪੰਜਾਬੀ) Sanskrit (संस्कृत) Santali (संताली) Sindhi (سنڌي) Tamil (தமிழ்) Telugu (తెలుగు) Urdu (اردو) Powered by इस अनुवाद का मूल्यांकन करें क्या आप प्रतिक्रिया देना चाहेंगे जमा करें इस पृष्ठ का अनुवाद भाषिणी के ए. आई.-संचालित मॉडल का उपयोग करके किया गया है। स्रोत सामग्री अंग्रेजी में है। अनुवाद से संबंधित किसी भी प्रश्न के लिए, कृपया ceo-dibd@digitalindia.gov.in से संपर्क करें।

pages.title: "Nari Shakti: Women Empowerment through Entrepreneurship & Leadership" workshop, conducted at ni-msme. Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME | Workshops | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: "Nari Shakti: Women Empowerment through Entrepreneurship & Leadership" workshop, conducted at ni-msme. Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME | Workshops | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Workshops "Nari Shakti: Women Empowerment through Entrepreneurship & Leadership" workshop, conducted at ni-msme. Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of Ministry of MSME 11 June 2026 Mr. Rupesh Kumar 50 The "Nari Shakti: Women Empowerment through Entrepreneurship & Leadership" workshop, sponsored by IDBI Bank, was held on 11 June 2026 at the ni-msme campus. The programme witnessed participation from 120 women entrepreneurs, professionals, aspiring entrepreneurs, and small business owners. Officials from 12 banks shared women-centric financial support schemes and funding opportunities to promote entrepreneurship and leadership among women. nimsme MSMEs Entrepreneurship Workshop Nari Shakti News Categories Advanced Management Development Programs एटीआई पुस्तक प्रदर्शनी सहयोगात्मक कार्यक्रम उद्यमिता और कौशल विकास कार्यक्रम उद्यमिता जागरूकता कार्यक्रम आम खबरें हैकाथॉन उद्घाटन समारोह भारतीय तकनीकी और आर्थिक सहयोग कार्यक्रम (आई. टी. ई. सी.) अंतर्राष्ट्रीय सम्मेलन नौकरी मेला ऋण मेला प्रबंधन विकास कार्यक्रम समझौता ज्ञापन (एमओयू) राष्ट्रीय घोषित कार्यक्रम राष्ट्रीय सम्मेलन आधिकारिक दौरे पेटेंट प्रेस मीट एससी एसटी हब योजनाएं सेमिनार विशेष अवसर प्रायोजित कार्यक्रम प्रशिक्षकों का प्रशिक्षण Valediction Workshops Hindi (हिन्दी) English Assamese (অসমীয়া) Bengali (বাংলা) Bodo (बड़ो) Dogri (डोगरी) Goan Konkani (गोवा कोंकणी) Gujarati (ગુજરાતી) Hindi (हिन्दी) Kannada (ಕನ್ನಡ) Kashmiri (کٲشُر) Maithili (मैथिली) Malayalam (മലയാളം) Manipuri (মণিপুরী) Marathi (मराठी) Nepali (नेपाली) Odia (ଓଡ଼ିଆ) Punjabi (ਪੰਜਾਬੀ) Sanskrit (संस्कृत) Santali (संताली) Sindhi (سنڌي) Tamil (தமிழ்) Telugu (తెలుగు) Urdu (اردو) Powered by Rate this translation Would you like to provide feedback Submit इस पृष्ठ का अनुवाद भाषिणी के ए. आई.-संचालित मॉडल का उपयोग करके किया गया है। स्रोत सामग्री अंग्रेजी में है। अनुवाद से संबंधित किसी भी प्रश्न के लिए, कृपया ceo-dibd@digitalindia.gov.in से संपर्क करें।

pages.text: MSME Schemes | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) श्री जीतन राम मांझी माननीय केंद्रीय एमएसएमई मंत्री सुश्री शोभा करंदलाजे माननीय राज्य मंत्री, एमएसएमई एमएसएमई योजनाएं नवाचार, ग्रामीण उद्योग और उद्यमिता को बढ़ावा देने के लिए एक योजना (एस्पायर) प्रशिक्षण संस्थानों को सहायता (ए. टी. आई.) योजना कॉयर विकास योजना-अम्ब्रेला योजना सूक्ष्म और लघु उद्यमों के लिए क्रेडिट गारंटी फंड ट्रस्ट (सी. जी. टी. एम. एस. ई.) दबावग्रस्त एमएसएमई के लिए अधीनस्थ ऋण के लिए ऋण गारंटी योजना (सी. जी. एस. एस. डी.) प्रौद्योगिकी उन्नयन के लिए ऋण से जुड़ी पूंजी सब्सिडी योजना उद्यमिता और कौशल विकास कार्यक्रम (ईएसडीपी) योजना अंतर्राष्ट्रीय सहयोग (आईसी) योजना खादी ग्रामोद्योग विकास योजना-छतरी योजना सूक्ष्म और लघु उद्यम समूह विकास कार्यक्रम (एमएसई-सीडीपी) योजना एमएसएमई चैंपियंस योजना राष्ट्रीय एससी-एसटी हब योजना प्रधानमंत्री रोजगार सृजन कार्यक्रम (पीएमईजीपी) खरीद और विपणन सहायता (पी. एम. एस.) योजना पूर्वोत्तर क्षेत्र और सिक्किम में एमएसएमई को बढ़ावा देना एमएसएमई प्रदर्शन को बढ़ाना और तेज करना (आरएएमपी) पारंपरिक उद्योगों के पुनर्जनन के लिए निधि की योजना (स्फूर्ति) आत्मनिर्भर भारत (एस. आर. आई.) कोष आत्मनिर्भर भारत के लिए एमएसएमई को सशक्त बनाना उपकरण कक्ष और तकनीकी संस्थान-अवसंरचना विकास और क्षमता निर्माण का एक घटक हमारी पत्रिकाएँ लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार pages.title: SELF RELIANT INDIA (SRI) FUND Empowering MSMEs for Aatmanirbhar Bharat | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: SELF RELIANT INDIA (SRI) FUND Empowering MSMEs for Aatmanirbhar Bharat | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME SELF RELIANT INDIA (SRI) FUND Empowering MSMEs for Aatmanirbhar Bharat About the scheme: • India has embarked upon the path of self reliant economic growth for achieving its aspiration of becoming an economic superpower. Towards this end, one of the initiatives taken by the Government of India is the launch of the Self Reliant India (SRI) Fund. • The Fund structure is designed in a manner that it will leverage the strength of the private sector in providing growth capital to viable MSMEs having a definite growth plan. Fund Objective: The Fund aims to provide capital support to the Daughter Funds for onward provision to MSMEs as growth capital, through equity, quasi-equity and debt to achieve the following: • Supporting faster growth of MSME businesses, thereby igniting the economy & creating several employment opportunities. • Supporting enterprises which have the potential to graduate beyond the MSME bracket and become National/International champions. • Supporting MSMEs which will help make India self-reliant by producing relevant technologies, goods and services. pages.title: Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries(SFURTI) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries(SFURTI) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries(SFURTI) Objective: •To organize traditional industries and artisans into collectives by increasing production and value addition to make products competitive •To promote traditional sectors and increase income of artisans providing sustainable employment. Key Benefits: •GoI support: •Upto Rs. 2.5 cr. for upto 500 artisans •Rs. 5 cr for more than 500 artisans. •A production facility is set up with latest machineries •Raw material support •Soft Interventions – upto Rs. 25 lakhs •Skill Development •Exposure Visits •Buyer Seller Meets •Marketing connect, e-commerce •Design support Read more Our Journals लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) Featured News "Nari Shakti: Women Empowerment through Entrepreneurship & Leadership"... 11 June 2026 Aspire to Enterprise - Loan Mela conducted at ni-msme under ATI Scheme of... 11 June 2026 Ghana Delegation Led by Augustus Goosie Visits ni-msme to Explore MSME... 10 June 2026 ni-msme inaugurated A-MDP on Designing Strategic Interventions for Vibrant,... 08 June 2026 An Expert Level Train the Trainer workshop on Steam System Optimization... 08 June 2026 ni-msme Organised a Workshop on Strengthening MSME Online Dispute Resolution... 04 June 2026 Hindi (हिन्दी) English Assamese (অসমীয়া) Bengali (বাংলা) Bodo (बड़ो) Dogri (डोगरी) Goan Konkani (गोवा कोंकणी) Gujarati (ગુજરાતી) Hindi (हिन्दी) Kannada (ಕನ್ನಡ) Kashmiri (کٲشُر) Maithili (मैथिली) Malayalam (മലയാളം) Manipuri (মণিপুরী) Marathi (मराठी) Nepali (नेपाली) Odia (ଓଡ଼ିଆ) Punjabi (ਪੰਜਾਬੀ) Sanskrit (संस्कृत) Santali (संताली) Sindhi (سنڌي) Tamil (தமிழ்) Telugu (తెలుగు) Urdu (اردو) Powered by इस अनुवाद का मूल्यांकन करें क्या आप प्रतिक्रिया देना चाहेंगे जमा करें इस पृष्ठ का अनुवाद भाषिणी के ए. आई.-संचालित मॉडल का उपयोग करके किया गया है। स्रोत सामग्री अंग्रेजी में है। अनुवाद से संबंधित किसी भी प्रश्न के लिए, कृपया ceo-dibd@digitalindia.gov.in से संपर्क करें।

pages.title: Procurement and Marketing Support (PMS) Scheme | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Procurement and Marketing Support (PMS) Scheme | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Procurement and Marketing Support (PMS) Scheme Objective: •The scheme aims to promote new market access initiatives like organizing / participation in National / International Trade Fairs / Exhibitions / MSME Expo, etc. held across the country and to create awareness and educate the MSMEs about the importance / methods/ process of packaging in marketing, latest packaging technology, import-export policy and procedure, GeM portal, MSME Conclave, latest developments in international / national trade and other subjects / topics relevant for market access developments. pages.title: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) Availability of bank credit without the hassles of collaterals / third party guarantees would be a major source of support to the first generation entrepreneurs to realise their dream of setting up a unit of their own Micro and Small Enterprise (MSE). Keeping this objective in view, Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of India launched Credit Guarantee Scheme (CGS) so as to strengthen credit delivery system and facilitate flow of credit to the MSE sector. To operationalise the scheme, Government of India and SIDBI set up the Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE). CGTMSE has introduced a new "Hybrid Security" product allowing guarantee cover for the portion of credit facility not covered by collateral security. In the partial collateral security model, the MLIs will be allowed to obtain collateral security for a part of the credit facility, whereas the remaining part of the credit facility, up to a maximum of 200 lakh, can be covered under Credit Guarantee Scheme of CGTMSE. CGTMSE will, however, have pari-passu charge on the primary security as well as on the collateral security provided by the borrower for the credit facility. The main objective is that the lender should give importance to project viability and secure the credit facility purely on the primary security of the assets financed. The other objective is that the lender availing guarantee facility should endeavor to give composite credit to the borrowers so that the borrowers obtain both term loan and working capital facilities from a single agency. The Credit Guarantee scheme (CGS) seeks to reassure the lender that, in the event of a MSE unit, which availed collateral free credit facilities, fails to discharge its liabilities to the lender, the Guarantee Trust would make good the loss incurred by the lender up to 50/75/80/85 per cent of the credit facility. pages.title: About us | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: About us | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Vision To play an active role in MSME policy formulation and implementation in priority sectors aligned with Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Mission To emerge as Centre of Excellence in Innovation and Entrepreneurship for sustainable growth of MSMEs while conforming to national priorities for attaining global standards. About ni-msme ni-msme , the pioneer institute of MSME , is playing a transformational role in providing a pro-business environment to foster the progress of MSMEs . The raison detre of this Institute is to assist the Government in formulating policies for micro, small and medium enterprises and to help the practicing and potential entrepreneurs through a bouquet of services like training, research, consultancy, information, education and extension. The Institute is a training ground for middle and senior level technocrats, bureaucrats, bankers, potential entrepreneurs, trainers, educationists and Civil Society Organizations who come here to gain knowledge and expertise for keeping abreast of cutting-edge management practices and streamline their operations. Set up in 1960, ni-msme has made valuable contributions by creating an impressive record of achievements beyond the Indian shores, enabling other developing countries to access the benefit of the Institute’s expertise and facilities. ni-msme’s initiatives are dynamic and futuristic for meeting the needs of enterprises in the context of changing Global Economic Scenarios. Located in a sprawling and enthralling campus amidst a rich natural setting in Hyderabad, ni-msme is well equipped with physical and academic infrastructure. In keeping with the changing times and technological advances, the Institute has revolutionized its style of functioning by adopting technology in every facet of its mandate, even while retaining the wisdom and advantages of deeply ingrained management practices. Our Incubation Center nucleates a cluster of new age ventures. The Institute collects and disseminates information that helps make a successful entrepreneur who is well versed in the intricacies of business and can participate in business activities intelligently and diligently. The Small Enterprises National Documentation Centre (SENDOC) facilitates this process. The institute has recently commemorated the Diamond Jubilee, 60 years of commendable services to MSME sector and the country. The Director General Dr. Ashutosh A. pages.title: Our Genesis | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Our Genesis | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Our Genesis 2022 Diamond Jubilee The institute commemorated its Diamond Jubilee, 60 years of commendable services to MSME sector and the country. 2012 Golden Jubilee The Institute celebrated its Golden Jubilee Year. 2007 Rechristened as ni-msme The Institute has earned distinction in entrepreneurship promotion, with recognition at national and international levels. With the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Development Act of 2006 (MSME-D) the Institute was renamed as National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) with re-designed innovative focus of inclusive growth under the Ministry of MSME. 1984 UNIDO Recognition The UNIDO recognizing the outstanding performance of SIET under the scheme of Centers of Excellence, for its meritorious performance accorded the national status and renamed as National Institute for Small Industries Extension and Training (nisiet) in 1984. 1962 Renaming the Institute The Institute was renamed as Small Industry Extension Training Institute (SIET) shifting the headquarters to Hyderabad in 1962. 1960 The Foundation Central Industrial Extension Training Institute (CIETI) took birth with Micro Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) as a Department under the Ministry of Industry and Commerce in New Delhi. In order to provide greater autonomy in administration and procedures to play a pivotal role in the promotion of MSMEs. pages.title: Dr. Ashutosh A. Murkute | Director General | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Dr. Ashutosh A. Murkute | Director General | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Director General Dr. Ashutosh A. Murkute Director General Profile Blog posts Publications Awards Profile Dr. Ashutosh A. Murkute, assumed the charge of In-charge Director General, National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) on 19 March 2025. After completing Ph.D. from I.I.T., Delhi he started research career as Scientist ‘C’ at DRDO – Defence Institute of High Altitude Research (DIHAR, DRDO), Leh-Ladakh. Besides developing agro-technologies suitable for high altitude, he established a prototype for solar thermal greenhouse and an experimental tissue culture lab at Leh. Dr. Murkute (Senior Scientist), worked on post-harvest management of onion and garlic at ICAR – Directorate of Onion and Garlic Research, Pune. He established a protocol for cold – storage of onion vis-à-vis inherent potential of different cultivars. He also developed a complete protocol for production of virus-free planting material of garlic through microbulbiles using mericlones and established a tissue culture lab for the mass production. At ICAR – Central Citrus Research Institute, Nagpur, Dr. Murkute (Principal Scientist), worked on citrus improvement program and also projects on abiotic stress management. His team released three citrus varieties i.e. pummelo (Citrus grandis) cv. NRCC Pummelo-5, grapefruit (Citrus paradisi) cv. Flame and Sweet Orange (Citrus sinensis) cv. Cutter Valencia through State Variety Release Committee (SVRC), Maharashtra and a grapefruit (Citrus paradisi) cv. NRCC Grapefruit -6 through institute for commercial cultivation. He published research articles in high rated peer reviewed journals of international repute on high-density panting, organic cultivation, tissue culture etc. There are many books, book-chapters, popular articles etc. to his credit. As a Chairman of Works Committee he completed civil works of Rs. 500 lakhs and through project of revolving fund scheme he earned revenue of more than Rs 700 lakhs by selling quality disease-free citrus plants of acid lime, mandarin, sweet orange and other improved citrus cultivars at CCRI, Nagpur. As PI/ Co-PI of externally funded competitive grants he handled research project of more than Rs 3000 lakhs, besides institute funded projects. Appointments such as Adjunct Professor at I.I.T. Delhi; Member, Board of Management of Rajmata Vijaya Raje Scindia Agricultural University, Gwalior; Member, Academic Council of Nagpur University, Member, Management Committees of ICAR institutes’, Member, Board of Studies, Central Agriculture University, Imphal are some of the prestigious recognitions for his illustrious career. pages.text: Library | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Close Library The Small Enterprises National Documentation Centre (SENDOC) Library of ni-msme is a place where books, magazines, newspapers, and other materials are kept for people to read, borrow, or use. SENDOC is not just repository of books, but also a place of learning, research and community engagement which includes Collections - Wide range of collections that include books, magazines, newspapers and digital materials. Also have special collections that focus on Entrepreneurship and MSME. pages.title: Digital Classrooms | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Digital Classrooms | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Close Digital Classrooms Digital classrooms have become a popular way of learning, especially during the current pandemic. Here are some ideas for content that can be used in digital classrooms: Interactive quizzes and assessments - These can be used to test students' knowledge and understanding of a particular subject. They can also be used for formative assessments and to identify areas where students may need additional support. Video lectures and tutorials - These can be used to provide students with a more engaging and visual way of learning. They can be used to introduce new topics, reinforce concepts, or provide additional explanations. Virtual field trips - These can be used to take students on a virtual tour of a particular location or to experience a particular event. They can be used to provide students with a more immersive learning experience and to help them develop a deeper understanding of a particular subject. Collaborative projects - These can be used to encourage students to work together and to develop important skills such as communication, collaboration, and problem-solving. Projects can be based on a particular topic or theme and can be completed using online tools such as Google Docs or Padlet. Digital textbooks and resources - These can be used to provide students with access to a wide range of learning materials, including textbooks, articles, and videos. They can be used to supplement classroom instruction and to provide students with additional resources and materials to support their learning. These are just a few ideas for content that can be used in digital classrooms. By using a variety of different formats and approaches, teachers can create engaging and effective learning experiences for their students.
