pages.title: Recreation | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Recreation | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Close Recreation Recreation facilities at ni-msme are essential for promoting health and wellness among participants, students and staff. Here are some common recreation facilities that can be found at ni-msme : Fitness centre: ni-msme have fitness center that is equipped with weight training equipment, cardio machines, and group fitness classes. These facilities are designed to help students and staff maintain an active and healthy lifestyle. Sports courts and fields: ni-msme campus provides Badminton Court, Tennis Court, Football Ground for organized sports teams or for casual play that allow students and staff to participate in organized sports leagues and tournaments. A great way to stay active and meet new people on campus. Recreation facilities are important for promoting physical health and wellness, as well as providing a sense of community and social engagement. By providing access to these facilities, ni-msme can help students and staff lead healthy and fulfilling lives both on and off campus. हमारी पत्रिकाएँ लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) प्रमुख समाचार "नारी शक्तिः उद्यमिता और नेतृत्व के माध्यम से महिला सशक्तिकरण" 11 June 2026 उद्यम की आकांक्षा-ए. टी. आई. योजना .......... के तहत एन. आई. एम. एस. एम. ई. में आयोजित ऋण मेला 11 June 2026 ऑगस्टस गूसी के नेतृत्व में घाना के प्रतिनिधिमंडल ने एमएसएमई का पता लगाने के लिए एनआई-एमएसएमई का दौरा किया 10 June 2026 एन. आई.-एम. एस. एम. ई. ने वाइब्रेंट के लिए रणनीतिक हस्तक्षेप तैयार करने पर ए-एम. डी. पी. का उद्घाटन किया 08 June 2026 एक विशेषज्ञ स्तर पर प्रशिक्षक कार्यशाला को भाप प्रणाली अनुकूलन पर प्रशिक्षित करें 08 June 2026 एन. आई.-एम. एस. एम. ई. ने एम. एस. एम. ई. ऑनलाइन विवाद समाधान को मजबूत करने पर एक कार्यशाला का आयोजन किया 04 June 2026 Hindi (हिन्दी) English Assamese (অসমীয়া) Bengali (বাংলা) Bodo (बड़ो) Dogri (डोगरी) Goan Konkani (गोवा कोंकणी) Gujarati (ગુજરાતી) Hindi (हिन्दी) Kannada (ಕನ್ನಡ) Kashmiri (کٲشُر) Maithili (मैथिली) Malayalam (മലയാളം) Manipuri (মণিপুরী) Marathi (मराठी) Nepali (नेपाली) Odia (ଓଡ଼ିଆ) Punjabi (ਪੰਜਾਬੀ) Sanskrit (संस्कृत) Santali (संताली) Sindhi (سنڌي) Tamil (தமிழ்) Telugu (తెలుగు) Urdu (اردو) Powered by इस अनुवाद का मूल्यांकन करें क्या आप प्रतिक्रिया देना चाहेंगे जमा करें इस पृष्ठ का अनुवाद भाषिणी के ए. आई.-संचालित मॉडल का उपयोग करके किया गया है। स्रोत सामग्री अंग्रेजी में है। अनुवाद से संबंधित किसी भी प्रश्न के लिए, कृपया ceo-dibd@digitalindia.gov.in से संपर्क करें।

pages.title: School of Enterprise Development (SED) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: School of Enterprise Development (SED) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME School of Enterprise Development (SED) About School It is one of the former scholastic/intellectual departments of ni-msme, continued with additional responsibilities in the globalised market environment. MSME development generally refers to facilitating progress in the economy and generation of employment by enhancing business development services, and establishing a conducive legal, regulatory and policy environment. In this context, the School of Enterprise Development [SED] acts as a catalyst for dynamic, integrated change in Micro and Small Enterprise (MSE) sector, looking strategically and optimistically towards the future challenges. The activities of the SED include programme evaluation studies, research projects, feasibility studies, potential surveys, rural enterprise promotion, backward area development, infrastructure development programme and project implementation for central and state governments, that advances an innovative and enterprise growth in the global and national scenario. On the international front, the SED provides a bridge between theory and practice of small business and MSE development by guiding/training the executives of MSME in formulation, development, implementation and evaluation of various facets of enterprise policy, with a focus to make the MSE sector more competitive in the global economic scene. Besides imparting training to the officials, the experienced and well-known faculty of the school focus on economic surveys in the nature of diagnosing the industrial potential, networking with national and international agencies, and other research studies pertaining to government programmes /schemes and impact studies. The School of Enterprise Development functions through Centre for Enterprise Planning and Development (C-EPD), Centre for Policy Research (C-PR), National Resource Centre for Cluster Development (NRCD), ni-msme NGO Network (N-Cube) and Economic Investigation and Statistical Cell (EISC) in the areas of Cluster development, Employment Generation, and establishing synergy with related departments, which of late became the centres of excellence through focused attention for holistic development. pages.title: School of Enterprise Management (SEM) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: School of Enterprise Management (SEM) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME School of Enterprise Management (SEM) About School This is a well-established academic organ of ni-msme. There is a spate of changes sweeping the enterprises across the globe. The economic reforms within the country with the attendant consequences, ever increasing competition, both global and local, fast pace of technological changes and a host of other factors necessitate revolutionary reforms in the enterprise management practices. The practices clearly have to be cost-effective as well as simpler to adopt and adapt with the power to impinge all segments of the enterprise. This calls for a paradigm shift in raison d’etre of the enterprise itself. In tune with this, the erstwhile Industrial Management Division is re-engineered and re-christened as School of Enterprise Management (SEM) laying emphasis on management practices, intellectual property rights and management education. The School is endowed with capabilities in the following areas and delivers through the instruments of training and consultancy, many a time customised to meet the requirements of client organisation, actively supported by research. The School functions with its full-fledged five centres viz., Centre for Promotion of Advanced Management Practices (C-PAMP), Centre for Logistics & Integrated Materials Systems (C-LAIMS), Centre for Industrial Credit and Financial Services (C-ICFS) and Centre for Intellectual Property Rights (C-IPR) and Centre for Environment Concerns (C-Eco). pages.title: School of Entrepreneurship and Extension (SEE) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: School of Entrepreneurship and Extension (SEE) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) श्री जीतन राम मांझी माननीय केंद्रीय एमएसएमई मंत्री सुश्री शोभा करंदलाजे माननीय राज्य मंत्री, एमएसएमई उद्यमिता और विस्तार विद्यालय (एस. ई. ई.) स्कूल के बारे में स्कूल ऑफ एंटरप्रेन्योरशिप एंड एक्सटेंशन समकालीन एमएसएमई प्राथमिकताओं के साथ क्षमता निर्माण, नीति आउटरीच और जमीनी स्तर पर उद्यमिता को बढ़ावा देने के लिए एक गतिशील केंद्र के रूप में विकसित हुआ है। स्कूल एमएसएमई नीति कार्यान्वयन, व्यवसाय विकास रणनीतियों, विपणन, परियोजना योजना और उद्यमिता को शामिल करते हुए अभ्यास-उन्मुख, बहु-अनुशासनात्मक प्रशिक्षण कार्यक्रमों को डिजाइन करने और वितरित करने में सक्रिय रूप से लगा हुआ है, जो अक्सर नवाचार पारिस्थितिकी तंत्र के साथ एकीकृत होता है। इसके अलावा, एस. ई. ई. ने उद्यमिता जागरूकता, आउटरीच कार्यक्रमों और संस्थागत सहयोग में महत्वपूर्ण भूमिका निभाई है, जिसमें एमएसएमई पारिस्थितिकी तंत्र को मजबूत करने और विशेष रूप से युवाओं, महिलाओं, एसएचजी और हाशिए पर रहने वाले समुदायों के बीच समावेशी विकास को बढ़ावा देने के लिए राज्य एजेंसियों और वित्तीय संस्थानों के साथ साझेदारी शामिल है। स्कूल के हस्तक्षेप उद्यमिता विकास और स्थानीय उद्यम निर्माण पर तेजी से जोर देते हैं, जिसमें मजदूरी रोजगार और सूक्ष्म उद्यम दोनों पैदा करने के लिए जिलों में प्रशिक्षण बैच आयोजित किए जा रहे हैं। इसके अलावा, एस. ई. ई. विशेषज्ञ व्याख्यानों, सम्मेलनों और परामर्श कार्यक्रमों के माध्यम से राष्ट्रीय और अंतर्राष्ट्रीय ज्ञान के प्रसार में योगदान देता है, जो नवाचार प्रसार, प्रौद्योगिकी हस्तांतरण और सतत उद्यम विकास जैसे उभरते क्षेत्रों को संबोधित करता है। इसके कार्यक्रमों की विशेषता संवादात्मक शिक्षाशास्त्र, मामले-आधारित शिक्षा, क्षेत्र में भागीदारी, व्यावहारिक परिणाम सुनिश्चित करना और संस्थागत क्षमताओं में वृद्धि है। कुल मिलाकर, एस. ई. ई. उद्यमशीलता की क्षमताओं को बढ़ावा देकर, शासन क्षमताओं को मजबूत करके और समावेशी और टिकाऊ एम. एस. एम. ई. विकास को सक्षम करके "विकसित भारत" के दृष्टिकोण के साथ अपनी गतिविधियों को संरेखित करना जारी रखता है। इसके घटक केंद्र इस प्रकार हैंः 1. उद्यमिता विकास प्रकोष्ठ 2. आजीविका व्यवसाय ऊष्मायन प्रकोष्ठ निर्देशक डॉ. श्रीकांत शर्मा निर्देशक संकाय श्री वी. बी. राजेंद्र प्रसाद संकाय सदस्य डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार संकाय सदस्य श्री गुडेल देवन साई फैकल्टी एसोसिएट कार्यक्रम सभी कार्यक्रम आने वाला है जारी है ग्रामीण उद्यमों के लिए मूल्य श्रृंखला विकास पर प्रशिक्षण कार्यक्रम डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार 22 June 2026 ऑनलाइन वैज्ञानिक मधुमक्खी पालन पर प्रशिक्षण कार्यक्रम तेल के बीजों के साथ एकीकृत डॉ. श्रीकांत शर्मा 18 June 2026 नी-मिमे अभिनव शिक्षण और सीखने पर ऑनलाइन संकाय विकास कार्यक्रम (एफ. डी. पी.) डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार 01 September 2025 ऑनलाइन टिकाऊ .......... के लिए प्रभावी कार्यशील पूंजी प्रबंधन और ऋण संबंध डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार 28 July 2025 ऑनलाइन उद्यमिता और कौशल विकास में प्रशिक्षकों (टीओटी) का प्रशिक्षण डॉ. श्रीकांत शर्मा 21 July 2025 नी-मिमे ग्रामीण क्षेत्रों के कायाकल्प के लिए सूक्ष्म उद्यमों पर अंतर्राष्ट्रीय प्रशिक्षण कार्यक्रम डॉ. श्रीकांत शर्मा 23 February 2025 नी-मिमे स्टार्टअप को सशक्त बनानाः नवाचार, विकास और विकास के लिए एमएसएमई योजनाओं में महारत हासिल करना डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार 20 February 2025 ऑनलाइन भारत में किसान उत्पादक कंपनियों (एफ. पी. सी.) को सशक्त बनानाः .......... के माध्यम से विकास को चालू करना डॉ. सत्येंद्र कुमार 20 January 2025 ऑनलाइन समाचार श्रेणियाँ उन्नत प्रबंधन विकास कार्यक्रम एटीआई पुस्तक प्रदर्शनी सहयोगात्मक कार्यक्रम उद्यमिता और कौशल विकास कार्यक्रम उद्यमिता जागरूकता कार्यक्रम आम खबरें हैकाथॉन उद्घाटन समारोह भारतीय तकनीकी और आर्थिक सहयोग कार्यक्रम (आई. टी. ई. सी.) अंतर्राष्ट्रीय सम्मेलन नौकरी मेला ऋण मेला प्रबंधन विकास कार्यक्रम समझौता ज्ञापन (एमओयू) राष्ट्रीय घोषित कार्यक्रम राष्ट्रीय सम्मेलन आधिकारिक दौरे पेटेंट प्रेस मीट एससी एसटी हब योजनाएं सेमिनार विशेष अवसर प्रायोजित कार्यक्रम प्रशिक्षकों का प्रशिक्षण मोक्ष प्राप्ति कार्यशालाएँ हमारी पत्रिकाएँ लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और pages.title: School of Enterprise Information and Communication (SEIC) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: School of Enterprise Information and Communication (SEIC) | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME School of Enterprise Information and Communication (SEIC) About School The School of Information and Communication (SEIC) is a well established unique knowledge hub of ni-msme in providing information services and ICT solutions through training to both the national and international MSME community. To strengthen the informational base of MSMEs in facing the global challenges led to the formation of School of Enterprise Information and Communication (SEIC) laying emphasis on providing information services in varied forms and need based skill oriented Information Technology (IT) training using the latest technologies. The school functions with its fully equipped sources of information and state-of-the-art infrastructure to meet the informational needs and ICT skill development training requirements of MSMEs, Government organisations, public and private sectors, NGOs, entrepreneurs, students, researchers etc. through its two Centres viz, Small Enterprises National Documentation Centre (SENDOC) and Centre for Communication and Information Technology (C-CIT). pages.title: National Resource Centre for Cluster Development (NRCD) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: National Resource Centre for Cluster Development (NRCD) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME National Resource Centre for Cluster Development (NRCD) The National Resource Centre for Cluster Development (NRCD), established in 2004 at ni-msme with support from the Development Commissioner (MSME), was created to strengthen the competitiveness of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) through a cluster-based development approach. This approach addresses key challenges faced by MSMEs such as isolation, lack of technology, weak market linkages, and limited access to skills and information. The primary objective of NRCD is to promote and implement cluster development across India by providing end-to-end support to MSME clusters. Its core functions include cluster identification, diagnostic studies, preparation of detailed project reports, strategic intervention design, capacity building, and continuous monitoring and handholding. It also facilitates technology upgradation, infrastructure development, and deployment of business development services to improve productivity and competitiveness. NRCD plays a crucial role in implementing government initiatives like the SFURTI scheme, supporting multiple clusters across states. Through these interventions, it enhances artisan incomes, promotes innovation, strengthens market access, and ensures sustainable enterprise development. Overall, NRCD acts as a national-level catalyst for transforming MSMEs into competitive, collaborative, and growth-oriented ecosystems. pages.title: Intellectual Property Facilitation Centre (IPFC) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Intellectual Property Facilitation Centre (IPFC) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Intellectual Property Facilitation Centre (IPFC) The Intellectual Property Facilitation Center (IPFC) at the National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) , Hyderabad, is a dedicated resource center established to assist MSMEs, startups, entrepreneurs, academic institutions, and innovators in leveraging the value of Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs). With the primary objective of strengthening the IP ecosystem in India, the IPFC at ni-msme provides end-to-end support for patents, trademarks, copyrights, industrial designs, and geographical indications . The center aims to promote awareness, build capacity, and facilitate protection and commercialization of innovations among MSMEs Key Services Offered : IP Awareness & Capacity Building : Organizing workshops, seminars, training programs, and webinars on IPR for MSMEs, startups, students, and faculty. IPR Consultancy : Guidance on patentability assessment, prior art search, filing strategies, trademark protection, and design registrations. Filing Support : End-to-end assistance in drafting and filing of patents, trademarks, copyrights, and design applications with Indian IP offices. Commercialization & Monetization : Advising on IP valuation, technology transfer, licensing, and strategies to generate revenue from IP assets. The IPFC operates under the guidance of experienced professionals, registered patent and trademark agents, and domain experts. It serves as a bridge between innovation and market realization by empowering MSMEs to protect and capitalize on their creative and innovative work. pages.title: Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) The Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) at the National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) is committed to fostering a vibrant and inclusive entrepreneurial ecosystem. The Cell plays a pivotal role in promoting entrepreneurship through comprehensive capacity building, mentoring, handholding support, and enterprise facilitation services for both aspiring and existing entrepreneurs. Objectives To promote entrepreneurship as a viable and sustainable career option among youth, women, and marginalized communities. To strengthen MSMEs through skill development, innovation, and enterprise support services. To facilitate adoption of technology-driven and environmentally sustainable business practices. Core Functions and Activities 1. Entrepreneurship Development Programmes (EDPs) EDC designs and delivers structured training programmes focusing on business planning, financial literacy, market linkages, regulatory compliance, and the use of digital tools for enterprise growth. 2. Entrepreneurship Awareness Programmes (EAPs) These programmes aim to sensitize students and potential entrepreneurs about emerging business opportunities, Government schemes, and the evolving startup ecosystem. 3. Skill Development & Capacity Building The Cell conducts sector-specific skill development initiatives to enhance employability and build entrepreneurial competencies aligned with market demand. 4. Incubation & Handholding Support EDC provides end-to-end support including idea validation, preparation of project reports, assistance in business registration, facilitation of access to finance, and continuous mentoring through incubation initiatives. 5. Facilitation of Government Schemes The Cell actively supports entrepreneurs in accessing various flagship schemes such as Prime Minister’s Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP), Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (MUDRA), Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) etc. 6. Innovation & Startup Promotion EDC promotes innovation-driven enterprises by supporting startups through mentoring, networking, and linkages with industry, academia, and research institutions. pages.title: Centre for Financial Inclusion (CFI), FinRise | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme)
pages.text: Centre for Financial Inclusion (CFI), FinRise | Centre of Excellence | National Institute for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme) Shri Jitan Ram Manjhi Hon'ble Union Minister for MSME Ms. Shobha Karandlaje Hon'ble Minister of State, MSME Centre for Financial Inclusion (CFI), FinRise Centre of Financial Inclusion is established at ni-msme with the support from SIDBI and CGTMSE. Objectives of CFI: To sensitise the CGTMSE & MLIs (Member Lending Institutions) on issues faced by MSEs at the field level. To create awareness on financial literacy and credit counselling to MSEs, thus helping them to better utilize the benefits of the CGTMSE Scheme. To disseminate knowledge on financial prudence and tailored best practices to Progressive entrepreneurs for a better financial mechanism. To develop formal and systematic credit avenues for the financially disadvantaged progressive entrepreneurs throughout their transformational journey to medium scale. Purpose Statement “To be Centre of Excellence in catering to MSEs needs to enhance their creditworthiness and promote Governance and financial management among MSEs through democratizing financial knowledge & best practices, inculcating financial discipline and prudence in MSEs ecosystem”. Role of SIDBI As principal Financial Institution for executing the triple agenda of promotion, financing and development of the MSME sector and coordination of the functions of the various Institutions engaged in similar activities. Role of CGTMSE To extend the support to progressive entrepreneurs who are having bankable business ideas but lack collateral security and/or third-party guarantee to access credit from formal source by helping them to provide the guarantee to enable them access credit leading to setting up viable micro and small enterprises. Thereby transforming them from job seekers to job providers and meaningfully contribute to nation building. Role of ni-msme Being a pioneer Entrepreneurship Development Institute, will establish a Chair in CFI and will carry out research and consultancy activities for identifying the credit gaps and need analysis for MSMEs and build the capacity of all stakeholders under CGTMSE spectrum for greater utilization of CGTMSE benefits. हमारी पत्रिकाएँ लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) लघु उद्यम विकास प्रबंधन और विस्तार (एसईडीएमई) प्रमुख समाचार "नारी शक्तिः उद्यमिता और नेतृत्व के माध्यम से महिला सशक्तिकरण" 11 June 2026 उद्यम की आकांक्षा-ए. टी. आई. योजना .......... के तहत एन. आई. एम. एस. एम. ई. में आयोजित ऋण मेला 11 June 2026 ऑगस्टस गूसी के नेतृत्व में घाना के प्रतिनिधिमंडल ने एमएसएमई का पता लगाने के लिए एनआई-एमएसएमई का दौरा किया 10 June 2026 एन. आई.-एम. एस. एम. ई. ने वाइब्रेंट के लिए रणनीतिक हस्तक्षेप तैयार करने पर ए-एम. डी. पी. का उद्घाटन किया 08 June 2026 एक विशेषज्ञ स्तर पर प्रशिक्षक कार्यशाला को भाप प्रणाली अनुकूलन पर प्रशिक्षित करें 08 June 2026 एन. आई.-एम. एस. एम. ई. ने एम. एस. एम. ई. ऑनलाइन विवाद समाधान को मजबूत करने पर एक कार्यशाला का आयोजन किया 04 June 2026 Hindi (हिन्दी) English Assamese (অসমীয়া) Bengali (বাংলা) Bodo (बड़ो) Dogri (डोगरी) Goan Konkani (गोवा कोंकणी) Gujarati (ગુજરાતી) Hindi (हिन्दी) Kannada (ಕನ್ನಡ) Kashmiri (کٲشُر) Maithili (मैथिली) Malayalam (മലയാളം) Manipuri (মণিপুরী) Marathi (मराठी) Nepali (नेपाली) Odia (ଓଡ଼ିଆ) Punjabi (ਪੰਜਾਬੀ) Sanskrit (संस्कृत) Santali (संताली) Sindhi (سنڌي) Tamil (தமிழ்) Telugu (తెలుగు) Urdu (اردو) Powered by इस अनुवाद का मूल्यांकन करें क्या आप प्रतिक्रिया देना चाहेंगे जमा करें इस पृष्ठ का अनुवाद भाषिणी के ए. आई.-संचालित मॉडल का उपयोग करके किया गया है। स्रोत सामग्री अंग्रेजी में है। अनुवाद से संबंधित किसी भी प्रश्न के लिए, कृपया ceo-dibd@digitalindia.gov.in से संपर्क करें।

M i c r o  S m a l l  a n d  M e d i u m  E n t e r p r i s e s  ( n i - m s m e )
An Organisation of Ministry of MSME, Govt. of India and ISO 9001:2015 Certified
Accredited as UTKRISHT / उ त्क ृ ष्ट , 3-Star Rating by Capacity Building Commission (CBC)
International Labour Organization (ILO) Women's Entrepreneurship (WE) Check Certified
The Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) are the undisputed  architects of India’s
journey toward a $5 trillion economy and the overarching vision of Viks it Bharat @ 2047. As
we transition into FY 2026-27, the MSME ecosystem is undergoing a pro found structural shift,
driven by digital integration, green manufacturing, and enhanced global competitiveness.
The Union Budget 2026-27 has furt her fortified this sector by placing MSMEs at the core of
India’s growth strategy. With the en hancement of Credit Guarantee covers to ₹ 10 crore, the
introduction of customized credit cards for micro-enterprises, and the operationalization of
the SME Growth Fund, the focus has shifted from mere survival to aggressive scaling and
equity support. Furthermore, initiatives like PM Vishwakarma and RAMP are bridging the gap
between traditional craftsmanship and modern industrial standards, ensuring an inclusive and
resilient value chain.
In alignment with these national priorities, ni-msme has curated the National Executive
Development Programmes for FY 2026-27. Our training calendar is meticulously designed to
address the emerging demands of I ndustry 5.0, including Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain in
supply chains, and Sustainable Practices like Green Hydrogen and ZED (Zero Defect Zero
Effect) certification. We aim to empower entrepreneurs, policymakers, and financial executives
with the tools to leverage platforms like ONDC and TReDS, facilitating seamless market access
and liquidity.
Our sprawling, world-class campus in Hyderabad continues to serve as a hub for innovation,
offering state-of-the-art academic infrastructure and a rich alumni network for global
experience sharing. This year, we are proud to present a comprehensive suite of over 68
capacity-building programs, ranging from IPR and Digital Marketing to Cluster Development
and International Trade.
I invite all stakeholders to engage with these programs and harness the expertise at ni-msme
to transform challenges into sustainable business advantages. Together, let us build a globally
competitive and innovation-led MSME sector.
Dr. Ashutosh A. Murkute
Director General
F o r e w o r d

About
The National Institute for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (ni-msme), Hyderabad, stands as a beacon
of entrepreneurial excellence, fostering innovation, capacity building, and sustainable business growth. As a
premier institution under the Ministry of Micro, Sm all & Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of Indi a,
ni-msme plays a pivotal role in shaping the future of India's entrepreneurial landscape. With a rich l egacy of
empowering enterprises, the institute is committed to enabling MSMEs to become competitive, resilient,
and globally integrated, in alignment with the Viksit Bharat 2047 vision.
At the heart of ni-msme’s mission is a dynamic appr oach to policy research, skill development, and
enterprise facilitation. The institute offers cutti ng-edge training programs, consultancy services, an d
strategic interventions that address the evolving n eeds of MSMEs. By integrating advanced technology,
sustainable practices, and global best standards, n i-msme nurtures a new generation of business leader s
who contribute to India’s transformation into a knowledge-driven economy.
With decades of expertise in entrepreneurship devel opment, financial management, digital transformatio n,
and global market integration, ni-msme stands as a center of excellence for MSMEs. The institute
specializes in policy advisory, capacity building, and research-driven innovation, ensuring enterprise s are
equipped with the necessary skills to thrive in a r apidly evolving economic landscape. By leveraging d igital
tools, artificial intelligence, and green technolog ies, ni-msme facilitates sustainable business pract ices that
align with India’s vision of becoming a developed economy by 2047.
In line with Viksit Bharat 2047, ni-msme envisions a future where MSMEs serve as the backbone of a sel f-
reliant and digitally empowered India. The institut e actively collaborates with national and internati onal
stakeholders to enhance market access, promote digi tal transformation, and foster inclusive growth.
Through industry-academia linkages, research initia tives, and policy advocacy, ni-msme ensures that
MSMEs are equipped to seize emerging opportunities in global trade and innovation.
The institute’s state-of-the-art infrastructure, ex perienced faculty, and an extensive network of indu stry
partnerships create an enabling ecosystem for enter prise development. With specialized programs in
financial literacy, export promotion, and technological advancement, ni-msme is committed to unlocking the
full potential of Indian businesses, making them gl obally competitive and future-ready. As India strid es
toward Viksit Bharat 2047, ni-msme remains steadfas t in its mission to drive entrepreneurial excellenc e,
economic empowerment, and inclusive development. By  fostering a culture of innovation, adaptability, a nd
sustainability, ni-msme continues to be a catalyst for transforming MSMEs into growth engines of a
progressive, self-sufficient, and globally recognized India.

ni-msme has four theme focused Schools of expertise, under which Centres of Excellence are
functioning synergistically to pursue specific activities. All th ese Centres have faculty expertise
and are equipped to conduct demand driven and customi zed collaborative programmes for the
client organizations. The faculty is ably supported by a group of Consultants, Technical &
Administrative Staff and empaneled Sectoral Experts.
The lush green campus with   accommodation annexes and study blocks, stretches of ve lvet
lawns and crowning trees, presents a picturesque setti ng easy on the eye and soothing to the
mind, making intellectual pursuit an enjoyable avocatio n. The dining complex contains well
maintained, spacious and air conditioned dining halls  equipped with modern kitchen facilities for
multi-cuisine cooking and serving which caters to 300 participants.
Functional Structure
At ni-msme, world-class training infrastructure seaml essly blends interactive learning with
cutting-edge applications. The institute houses two t raining complexes, namely “Training Block”
and “Diamond Jubilee Bhawan”, with the former one fea turing 14 well-equipped lecture halls, a
conference hall with a 125-seat capacity, and a state-of -the-art auditorium accommodating 300
participants. The “Diamond Jubilee Bhawan” was inaugurat ed in 2022 as part of its expansion
strategy, setting new benchmarks with industry-aligne d facilities, ensuring an immersive and
holistic learning experience consisting of 21 classrooms , 2 seminar halls – capacity of 150
participants each, and 1 computer lab. The central l ibrary, a treasure trove of 45,000 books and
8,200 journals, serves as an intellectual hub, while ded icated interaction rooms foster syndicate
discussions, brainstorming, and collaborative learning.  The entire training complex is Wi-Fi
enabled, providing seamless high-speed internet acce ss. Nestled in serene, sylvan
surroundings, the campus offers an enriching learning ambiance, where creativity and innovation
thrive at every corner. International participants un dergo a specialized induction program,
designed to blend Indian learning paradigms with cultu ral insights, ensuring deeper assimilation
of training concepts and their global applications. At ni-msme, excellence meets inspiration,
shaping future-ready leaders in the MSME ecosystem.

analysis.overview: The Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Technology Upgradation (CLCSS) is a government initiative aimed at facilitating technology upgradation in Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) by providing an upfront capital subsidy of 15% on institutional finance of up to Rs 1 crore availed by them. The scheme supports the induction of well-established and improved technology in specified 51 sub-sectors/products, enabling MSEs to upgrade plant and machinery with state-of-the-art technology, with or without expansion. It also covers new MSEs that have set up facilities with appropriate eligible and proven technology duly approved under scheme guidelines.
objectives: Provide upfront capital subsidy of 15% on institutional finance up to Rs 1 crore
Support induction of well-established and improved technology in 51 specified sub-sectors/products
Upgrade plant & machinery with state-of-the-art technology, with or without expansion
Support new MSEs that have set up facilities with approved eligible and proven technology
analysis.eligibility: Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) that have availed institutional finance of up to Rs 1 crore for technology upgradation in the specified 51 sub-sectors/products, using well-established and improved technology duly approved under scheme guidelines, including new MSEs that have set up facilities with appropriate eligible and proven technology.
analysis.benefits: Upfront capital subsidy of 15% on eligible institutional finance (up to Rs 1 crore), technology upgradation with state-of-the-art machinery, improved productivity and competitiveness, support for both existing and new MSEs adopting approved technology.
analysis.financial_support: The scheme provides an upfront capital subsidy of 15% on institutional finance availed by MSEs, with a maximum eligible loan amount of Rs 1 crore, resulting in a maximum subsidy of Rs 15 lakh per unit. The subsidy is disbursed directly to the lending institution upon verification of technology upgradation and loan disbursement.
analysis.application_process: 1. Avail institutional finance (term loan) from a bank or financial institution for technology upgradation. 2. Ensure the technology is among the 51 approved sub-sectors/products under CLCSS. 3. Submit application through the lending institution with required documents. 4. Lending institution forwards the claim to the nodal agency. 5. Nodal agency verifies eligibility and approves subsidy. 6. Subsidy amount (15% of loan, up to Rs 15 lakh) is released to the lending institution, which adjusts it against the borrower's loan account.
required_documents: Loan sanction letter from bank/financial institution
Invoice and payment proof for machinery/technology purchased
Details of technology adopted (specification, approval under 51 sub-sectors)
analysis.implementing_agency: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of India
caveats: Subsidy is limited to institutional finance of up to Rs 1 crore per unit
Only technologies listed in the 51 approved sub-sectors/products are eligible
Subsidy is available only for term loans, not working capital
Claim must be submitted within a specified period after loan disbursement and installation
Subsidy is adjusted against the loan account and not given as direct cash to the beneficiary
